contractSurface.factoryIdRange: not declared in current FLOW-38 contracts
contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped RAG feedback, retrieval outcome scoring, DPO promotion, distilled-rule extraction, and platform feedback dashboards
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-38 uses rag-quality-feedback as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug.
coreGuarantees: DPO triples are promoted to RAG only when validation and score thresholds are satisfied.
Cycle outcomes remain tenant scoped and each update carries the source cycleId.
Quality updates use idempotency keys before any score write.
Distilled rule extraction runs only for SUCCESS_WITHIN_BUDGET cycles.
RAG pattern, cycle outcome, and distilled rule writes remain behind database and queue fabrics.
Feedback widgets can be embedded across flows without exposing platform credentials.
Tenant scope remains automatic through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.
Vault credential paths remain outside forked packages and tenant docs.

machineLockedRules: no DPO triple means no classification and no downstream update
cycleId is stored with every outcome record
outcome record is stored before RagQualityUpdateRequested is enqueued
idempotency check for cycleId plus patternId happens before any score write
batch record is stored before PatternQualityUpdated is enqueued
extraction is blocked for non-success outcomes
each distilled rule carries the source cycleId
rule documents are stored before DistilledRuleSeeded is enqueued

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-38 values may tune module naming, reporting mode, feedback-window and retention policy, dashboard thresholds, pattern breakdown visibility, anonymous feedback policy, and rule-review windows without changing tenant isolation, fabric boundaries, classifier order, idempotency, outbox, or CloudEvents behavior.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to feedback-console naming, reporting mode, dashboard thresholding, retention and review windows, anonymous feedback policy, and display-level pattern/rule settings.

edgeCases.name: Acme RAG quality lab
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes FLOW-38 for strict internal feedback triage with short retention and audit-light reporting.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module naming, feedback window, dashboard threshold, retention, anonymous feedback policy
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Classifier thresholds, idempotency, tenant isolation, and outbox ordering remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind retrieval feedback hub
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes FLOW-38 for broader operations review with longer feedback history and pattern breakdowns enabled.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: report mode, feedback window, retention, pattern breakdown display
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Score writes remain idempotent and every update still records cycleId.

edgeCases.name: Tessera RAG learning loop
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes FLOW-38 for research/audit review with anonymous feedback enabled and longer rule-review windows.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: anonymous feedback policy, rule confidence, rule review window, reporting mode
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Distilled rule extraction still requires SUCCESS_WITHIN_BUDGET and store-before-event ordering.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T594 through T596 remain fixed for the runtime implementation bundle.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
CycleOutcomeClassifier does not classify when no DPO triple exists.
RagQualityUpdater checks idempotency before score writes.
Every quality update and distilled rule carries cycleId traceability.
Tenant scope is resolved through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.
Marketplace forks must use tenant credentials from Vault.
GitHub Actions and local registry readback are required cascade evidence.

tenantARagQualityLab.description: Tenant A tunes FLOW-38 for strict internal quality triage.
tenantARagQualityLab.impact: Acme gets a stricter RAG quality lab while classifier order, idempotency, and tenant isolation remain unchanged.
tenantARagQualityLab.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and package metadata record these values.

tenantBRetrievalFeedbackHub.description: Tenant B tunes FLOW-38 for broad operations feedback review.
tenantBRetrievalFeedbackHub.impact: Northwind gets a larger feedback hub while score updates remain idempotent.
tenantBRetrievalFeedbackHub.testable: Tenant B fork from Tenant A publishes a distinct package version and keeps CI green.

tenantCRagLearningLoop.description: Tenant C tunes FLOW-38 for research and audit review.
tenantCRagLearningLoop.impact: Tessera gets longer feedback/rule review windows and anonymous feedback while extraction and outbox invariants remain locked.
tenantCRagLearningLoop.testable: Tenant C fork from Tenant B adapts locally, passes package CI, and preserves auth boundaries.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: marketplace API as the only valid fork origin

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-38 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-38 package assembly copies the rag-quality-feedback implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
GitHub repo evidence screenshots and local registry publish/readback must exist at each cascade point.
